question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: throttleManager.throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the opcodeRateLimitBucket uses a greedy refill strategy (refillGreedy) that immediately restores tokens at the start of each second, by timing requests to the exact millisecond of bucket refill to submit two requests in rapid succession within the same second window, bypassing the intended 1 RPS limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request for a transaction that triggered a contract with a SLOAD-heavy loop (reading thousands of storage slots), causing the EVM replay with storage=true to load all storage slots from the database and serialize them into the response, exhausting both database connection pool and JVM heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the endpoint is a GET request with no CSRF protection or request body size limit, by sending requests with extremely large query parameter values for stack, memory, or storage (e.g., stack=truetruetrue...) that cause Spring's parameter binding to consume excessive memory during parsing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests for transactions that involve Hedera precompile calls (e.g., token operations), causing the EVM replay to trigger additional database queries for token state at the historical timestamp, multiplying the database load per request and exhausting the database connection pool shared across 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the EVM replay in ContractDebugService.processOpcodeCall() uses ContractCallContext.get() (a thread-local context), and by sending many concurrent requests, cause thread-local memory to accumulate across the thread pool, leading to gradual memory exhaustion that degrades node performance over time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a transactionIdOrHash containing special regex characters (e.g., backslashes, parentheses) that are processed by the TRANSACTION_ID_PATTERN or ETH_HASH_PATTERN regex in TransactionIdOrHashParameter.valueOf(), causing catastrophic backtracking in the regex engine and consuming 100% CPU on the parsing thread?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests for transactions that involve EVM contracts with large bytecode (up to the EVM bytecode size limit), causing the EVM replay to load and JIT-compile the bytecode on each replay, consuming excessive CPU and memory in the Besu EVM engine and degrading node throughput?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the opcodes endpoint performs no authentication check before calling properties.isEnabled(), meaning every request — even when the feature is disabled — still incurs the overhead of Spring MVC request parsing, path variable binding, and header extraction, enabling a low-cost request flood that exhausts the web server thread pool?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request for a transaction that triggered a contract emitting a very large number of LOG opcodes, causing the EVM replay to accumulate all log entries in memory during the trace, exhausting JVM heap and crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the requestTimeout is 10 seconds and the EVM replay is synchronous (blocking the HTTP thread), by sending requests for transactions that take close to 10 seconds to replay, causing the thread pool to fill with blocked threads and denying service to legitimate requests on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a valid transaction ID where the shard/realm/num values are at Long.MAX_VALUE boundaries, causing EntityId.of(shard, realm, num) in TransactionIdParameter.valueOf() to overflow or throw an uncaught exception that propagates as a 500 error, revealing internal stack traces or crashing the request handler?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the opcodes endpoint has no maximum response size enforcement, by targeting a transaction that produces a response of several gigabytes when stack=true&memory=true&storage=true, causing the gzip compression step itself to consume excessive CPU and memory before the response is sent, crashing the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests for transactions that involve cross-contract calls to many different contracts, causing the EVM replay to load historical state for each contract from the database, multiplying the number of database queries per request and exhausting the database connection pool, degrading all nodes sharing the same database?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the OpcodeContext is initialized with new ArrayList<>(opcodesSize) where opcodesSize = (int) params.getGas() / 3, and for a transaction with gasLimit=15000000, this pre-allocates an ArrayList of 5,000,000 capacity, consuming approximately 40MB of heap per request, enabling heap exhaustion with only ~25 concurrent requests on a node with 1GB heap?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request for a transaction that involved a contract with a tight infinite-loop-like pattern (e.g., JUMPDEST loop consuming all gas), causing the EVM replay to execute millions of opcodes before running out of gas, with each opcode being recorded in the OpcodeContext list when stack=true, producing a massive in-memory trace that exhausts heap?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the throttle check (throttleOpcodeRequest) is performed after the Accept-Encoding header validation, meaning a request with a valid gzip header but an invalid transaction ID still consumes a throttle token, allowing an attacker to exhaust the throttle bucket with cheap invalid requests and then submit expensive replays when the bucket refills?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests for transactions that involve Hedera token service precompile calls with large token metadata, causing the EVM replay to load and serialize all token state at the historical timestamp, producing responses that exhaust both database I/O and JVM heap memory on the node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the endpoint uses Spring MVC's synchronous request handling (not reactive/async), meaning each long-running EVM replay blocks a Tomcat worker thread, and by sending enough concurrent requests to fill the thread pool (default 200 threads), deny service to all other endpoints on the same node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a transactionIdOrHash that is a valid transaction ID format but with nanos value at exactly 999999999 (maximum), causing Instant.ofEpochSecond(seconds, nanos) in TransactionIdParameter.valueOf() to produce an unexpected timestamp that maps to a different database record than intended, potentially leaking data from adjacent transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the opcodes endpoint performs no input size validation on the transactionIdOrHash path variable, by sending a path variable of maximum URL length (e.g., 8192 characters), causing Spring's path variable binding and TransactionIdOrHashParameter.valueOf() to process an oversized string, consuming excessive memory and CPU?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests for transactions that involve EVM contracts using the EXTCODECOPY opcode to copy large amounts of external contract bytecode, causing the EVM replay to load and copy large bytecode arrays into memory at each opcode step, exhausting JVM heap when memory=true is set?
